Tests the assignment and execution of a function as a property of an object.

# Purpose
This code defines a simple function [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-010foo>) that returns the integer `1`. It creates an object `object` using the `Object` constructor and assigns the [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-010foo>) function to the `fun` property of `object`. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that calling `object.fun()` returns `1`, ensuring that the function assignment and invocation work as expected. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily demonstrating basic object manipulation and function assignment in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### object
- **Type**: ``Object``
- **Description**: An instance of the `Object` class that has a property `fun` assigned to the function `foo`. The `foo` function returns the number 1.
- **Use**: Stores a reference to the `foo` function in the `fun` property, allowing it to be called as `object.fun()`.
